commentary: Delighted to share that my abstract on community birth outcomes in rural Connemara has been accepted for the INMO Western Branch Annual Midwifery Conference 2027. The data on day-5 postnatal gaps in rural areas tells a story that needs to be heard. Looking forward to presenting in March 🌊 #Midwifery #INMO #CommunityMidwifery #RuralHealth #Ireland

Last week's HSE West Birth Centre public consultation at Galway City Hall was a powerful reminder of why community-led maternity care matters. Standing room only — and the stories from families in Connemara made the case better than any evidence paper. Thank you to everyone who came out. The work continues. #BirthCentre #Galway #Midwifery #HSEWest #MaternityServices

Just registered for NRP advanced recertification in January — and back into the NUIG Masters Module 4 in March. Continuous learning is a non-negotiable in this work. If you're a community midwife in the west of Ireland and want to connect. #NRP #NeonatologyTraining #Midwifery #CPD #NUIG #UniversityOfGalway

Hampton's Rest B&B is open for October and November bookings — Connemara at its most spectacular in autumn. Sky Road walks, fresh Atlantic air, and a full Irish breakfast waiting. 🍂 #Clifden #Connemara #BandB #WestOfIreland

Reflecting on five years as a community midwife with HSE West. The work is challenging, emotionally demanding, and absolutely irreplaceable. Grateful for every family who trusted me with their care. #Midwifery #HSEWest #CommunityHealth
NUIG Masters Module 3 research proposal submitted — exploring postnatal support gaps in rural Co. Galway. Data collection phase begins next month. #ResearchMidwifery #NUIGalway #RuralHealth
The Sky Road in October is something else entirely. Sometimes the best CPD is stepping outside and remembering why we live here. 📍 Clifden, Co. Galway #SkyRoad #Connemara #WestOfIreland
Another successful water birth at home in rural Galway yesterday. These moments remind me why continuity of care matters so much. #CommunityMidwifery #HomeBirth #WaterBirth #Ireland

Birth Centre Campaign update: over 2,500 petition signatures and growing. Thank you to everyone who shared. The HSE West consultation submission window is open until December. #BirthCentre #Galway #MaternityServices

Proud to have completed the HSE Obstetric Emergencies Workshop this week. Essential skills, brilliant facilitators. Highly recommend to any midwife or midwifery student. #ObstetricEmergencies #CPD #Midwifery
Etsy shop update: new batch of handmade Connemara crafts now live at GalwayWorkshop. A small way to support local creativity from the west of Ireland. #Etsy #IrishCraft #GalwayMakers

INMO Western Branch Annual Midwifery Conference — an inspiring day of talks, networking, and advocacy. So important to have a strong professional voice for midwifery in Ireland. #INMO #MidwiferyConference #IrishNurses
Hampton's Rest Christmas packages now available — 2 nights B&B on the Sky Road with festive breakfast and Atlantic views. Booking link in bio. #Christmas #Clifden #BandB #Connemara
Halfway through the NUIG Masters — what I've learned about evidence-based midwifery practice that I wish I'd known five years ago. A thread 🧵 #Midwifery #Masters #NUIGalway

Grateful for the brilliant HSE West community midwifery team. Rural maternity care is often overlooked, but the families we serve couldn't be more deserving. #Midwifery #HeroesOfHealth #CommunityHealth
Long afternoon at the Salthill clinic today. Every antenatal review reminded me why continuity matters — the relief when a woman says 'oh good, you again'.
The Community Birth Centre proposal is inching towards a costed model. Grateful to the WG for keeping the evidence base honest.
Postnatal home visits are where I learn the most. The doorstep, the kettle on, the quiet questions no clinic room ever hears.
Reading The Midwives' Cauldron on the walk out along the Prom this evening. Best podcast in the profession.
NRP recert prep in full swing. Reassuring to know these algorithms live in muscle memory when it matters.
Reflecting today on Marilynne Robinson — 'nothing true can be said about God from a posture of defense'. It travels far beyond theology.
Community midwifery is not the shadow of hospital midwifery. It's a distinct, evidence-based model — that's what this proposal will show.
Six weeks into the Postnatal Home Visit Pathway pilot. Early qualitative feedback: women feel seen, not just checked.
Grateful to Tomás Ó Riordáin for steering the BC WG with such care. Change happens when someone holds the room.
Fionnuala Walsh — 15 years my senior in this work — pulled me aside today with the kind of feedback only a true mentor can give.
The MDT worked well this Wednesday. Case escalations quicker, notes cleaner. Small wins compound.
Delighted to share our BC evidence summary was accepted for the MAI Conference in Kilkenny (Feb 2027). Roll on the abstract deadline.
Applied for the Advanced NRP recert this week. Simulation training in Dublin next month.
Home visits in the west this afternoon — Barna, Furbo, Spiddal. Every road narrower than the last. Every welcome warmer.
Antenatal education class tonight covered the fourth trimester. The women had better questions than my textbooks have answers.
Kai on Sea Road with Nina — best flat white in Galway, best friend since Trinity.
Christmas Day in Clifden this year. My mother's kitchen, my father's turf fire, my brother's kids underfoot.
Reading Ann Patchett's Bel Canto for the third time. She earns every sentence.
Winter runs along the Prom feel like a small act of hope. Head up, coat on, keep going.
Postnatal Home Visit Pathway v4 out to WG for review. Aiming for HSE sponsor sign-off by mid-December.
Peer supervision this afternoon with Aoife — the value of reflective practice never gets old.
NUIG Masters cohort session tonight. Prof Kelleher's close read of Robinson: exquisite.
The Continuity of Carer pilot data are starting to look promising. Small numbers, big signal.
Salthill morning: 8 antenatal reviews, 2 postnatal, 1 late-booker. Time for a coffee, then home visits.
A quiet reminder to my colleagues in acute maternity: your work matters. Community holds the thread, but the thread runs through you.
Grateful for Dr. Roisin Carey's clinical leadership on the escalation protocol. Rare to have a consultant who listens like she does.
Watching Fiona (5) and Oscar (3) chase seagulls in Salthill this weekend. This is the childhood I hope every family gets.
Achill Island in February with Colin — wild sea, empty beach, quiet cottage. The kind of trip that resets a whole year.
The Postnatal Wellbeing channel we set up in Teams has taken off — safe space for midwives to talk about the hard shifts.
Bathroom refit at Hampton's Rest B&B in Clifden finally underway. Winter bookings holding steady.
Ryanair to Lanzarote booked for late January — small break before the NRP recert intensifies.
The reading list for this quarter: Macfarlane's Underland, Dillard's Pilgrim at Tinker Creek, Patchett's These Precious Days.
Yoga at the Salthill studio tonight. My back thanks me every time I go.
Ordered a Bon Iver LP for the record player. 22, A Million. Some albums earn their reissue.
The Doula pilot is being scoped with clear criteria this week. Cathal MacGabhann's PHN memo helped a lot.
Sunday batch cook: chickpea stew, roast squash, brown bread. Colin taking care of the coeliac-safe crackers.
Community Birth Centre catchment analysis complete. 3,200 births / year in scope. This is a real number.
Peer supervision with Bríd today: continuity of carer is not just kind, it's clinically better. The literature is clear.
Antenatal class tonight covered infant feeding — mixed feeding, expressed milk, tongue tie referral pathways. Real questions, real answers.
Applied for the NUIG summer school on qualitative research methods. Building the thesis muscle.
The Galway Market on Saturday morning — sourdough from Goya's, olives from the Spanish stall, a bunch of dahlias from the flower woman. Reset button pressed.
Colin's coeliac-friendly meal plan for the week is a small miracle. Roast squash and lentil dahl tonight.
Nina's wedding at Ballynahinch Castle in August is on my bookshelf as a hardcover deadline. Cannot wait.
Reflecting on why I love this work: it is the privilege of being present at threshold moments. Nothing else compares.
Postnatal Home Visit Pathway HSE presentation date confirmed: 15 December. Now to draft the deck.
Home visits along the Wild Atlantic Way — the drive alone is restorative.
The BC WG's proposal outline is coming together: catchment, evidence, model, cost, timeline, risks. Six-slide test coming Friday.
Community midwives holding the line for physiological birth. It matters.
Halfway through Robert Macfarlane's Underland. Every chapter opens a new geography.
Bought a new pair of Hoka runners for the winter. The Prom deserves proper footwear.
Colin's parents over for Sunday lunch. His mum's crumble is unbeatable — even the coeliac-friendly version.
Owen and Amy called last night — Fiona's insisting on a mermaid party for her sixth birthday in June. I am on cake decoration duty.
Winter light along Salthill Prom this evening — grey sea, gold clouds. Small mercies.
The Doula pilot proposal draft: transparent selection criteria, quarterly review, sponsor sign-off before roll-out.
A tough shift on call last night. Grateful for the team. Grateful for the outcome.
Roisin Carey's escalation protocol update: crisper, cleaner, safer. The MDT will feel it.
Reading Iris Murdoch again — The Sea, the Sea. Her cadence lives in me now.
Bon Iver's Holocene on repeat for the drive to Clifden this weekend. It fits the landscape.
PN Pathway pilot: 24 completed home visits, 2 GP referrals, 0 hospital readmissions. Early days, but promising.
The Continuity of Carer pilot midterm review next week. Bring the data, tell the story.
Kai Cafe morning with Nina. She's stressed about the wedding seating chart. I promised her honest feedback.
NUIG cohort essay due next Friday — 4,000 words on qualitative methods in maternal health research.
Watching the seagulls wheel over the Prom this morning. This city stitches itself into every quiet moment.
Hampton's Rest B&B — winter bookings holding steady. My mother's hospitality is a masterclass.
Peer supervision on the transfer criteria today. Sharpened the thresholds. Documented the rationale.
The MAI abstract deadline is 5 December. Slide deck is Draft 2. On track.
Robinson's Housekeeping still stops me in my tracks — the moral clarity, the sentence rhythm.
Home visit today to a first-time mother struggling with postnatal blues. Referred to the GP, arranged follow-up. She'll be okay.
The best midwifery care is unglamorous, patient, careful. It is the opposite of intervention theatre.
Achill Sunday walk planned for February. Keem Bay, the deserted village, and a late lunch at Ted Lavelle's.
Colin's Junior Cert class is preparing for exams. He's up late marking mocks. His patience with fifteen-year-olds is a form of vocation.
The Community Birth Centre proposal now includes a risk-adjusted care pathway diagram. Clean, defensible, testable.
The Galway winter is closing in. Rain sideways, wind honest. I love it.
Podcast recommendation for midwives: The Midwives' Cauldron episode on postnatal mental health — clinical, kind, essential.
Practising for the Connemara Half Marathon in April. Currently at 12k long runs, tempo Tuesdays, easy Fridays.
Reading list update: added Rebecca Solnit's Wanderlust for the running commute podcast alternative.
The clinic today was full of first-time mothers. The bright terror in their eyes. The relief when I say 'you're doing well'.
Grateful for Deirdre Moran — our on-call co-midwife. Steady in a storm, funny in the lull.
The Postnatal Wellbeing Teams channel had 40 posts this week. Colleagues supporting colleagues. That's the culture.
The Wild Atlantic Way is not a tourist route to us. It is our commute, our Sunday drive, our home.
Reading Annie Dillard for the third time this year — Teaching a Stone to Talk. Every essay a small furnace.
The Postnatal Home Visit Pathway will be presented to HSE West sponsors on 15 December. I am ready.
Peer supervision with Fionnuala today — she reminded me not to swallow feedback whole. Chew it. Discard the bones.
Salthill Sunday: Kai for brunch with Colin, then the Prom, then batch cook. This is the shape of a good day.
House viewings in Salthill next weekend. Two candidates. Colin has the spreadsheet. I have the doubts.
MAI Conference registration open. Kilkenny in February. Bringing the abstract, bringing the team.
The Community of Practice among community midwives across HSE West is one of the best things about this job.
Home visit today to a second-time mother recovering well. Baby feeding beautifully. She thanked me for the pathway. That will keep me going.
The BC evidence summary is 14 pages long. It reads well. It is defensible. It is honest.
Music for the drive home tonight: Iron & Wine's Our Endless Numbered Days. It never dates.
